message:
fix(indexer): null staged-pipeline counters when not rebuilding

The lexical rebuild pipeline owns the staged-merge and
staged-shard-build controllers. When the indexer is not in a rebuild
window — incremental scans, the small-N pass that runs immediately
after a rebuild finishes, or simply idle — the
`capture_lexical_rebuild_pipeline_runtime` helper fills in
zero/empty defaults for every staged_* counter because there's
nothing to read from the pipeline.

Surfacing those zeros directly through `IndexingProgress`'s JSON
snapshot looks identical to the issue #196 class of bug ("staged
worker pool is broken to zero workers_max"), and triages exactly
wrong (issue #201 was filed against this exact false-signal). The
operator wakes up, looks at the dashboard, sees `staged_merge_-
workers_max: 0`, and starts chasing a non-existent semaphore
sizing bug — when the real story is just "no rebuild is running."

Fix in `src/indexer/mod.rs`:

- New helper `staged_field_or_null(is_rebuilding: bool, value:
  usize) -> serde_json::Value` returns the underlying counter as
  a JSON number when `is_rebuilding` is true, otherwise
  `Value::Null`. Doc comment cites issue #201 so future readers
  understand why the mapping isn't a straight passthrough.
- Route the five `staged_merge_*` counters and the four
  `staged_shard_build_*` counters through `staged_field_or_null`.
- Update the two `*_controller_reason` fields so they only emit
  the non-empty string when both `is_rebuilding` is true and the
  underlying reason is non-empty; otherwise emit `null`.

The JSON shape stays stable — all the field names are identical —
so consumers that already check `if value !== null` (or
`is_some_and(|n| n > 0)`) work without changes. The only difference
is that "no rebuild active" now reads as `null` instead of `0`,
which matches the operator's mental model and lines up with how
the controller_reason fields already behaved.

Drive-by: `refactor/artifacts/20260425T184600Z-fifth-simplify/final_dashboard.md`
checks in the wrap-up dashboard for the fifth simplify loop —
ledger of all 10 pass commits, the verification commands that
were run, and the documented pre-existing fmt-check drift in
three test files that were intentionally left out of scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
